source.title: The hippocampal sharp wave-ripple in memory retrieval for immediate use and consolidation

confidence_rationale: High-quality Nature Reviews Neuroscience synthesis of SWR content and function. Covers distinct SWR types, dual retrieval/consolidation role of single events, and the multi-mode structure of the seed distribution. Directly relevant to MECH-285's salience-interaction and multi-modal-priority architecture questions.
failure_signatures: Individual SWRs can simultaneously serve retrieval (for immediate behavioural use) and consolidation (for offline schema update), suggesting a single event may participate in multiple priority channels rather than being slotted into one.
Distinct SWR types differentiable by representational content, behavioural state, and physiological features -- a single-weight-per-anchor priority scheme is insufficient.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Hippocampal SWRs serve both retrieval (for immediate behavioural use, including planning and decision-making) and consolidation (memory storage and schema update); single SWRs may participate in both functions. Different SWR types correspond to different cognitive functions, distinguishable by content, state, and physiology.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-285 asks whether the sleep consumer's start-state distribution has a single priority weighting. Joo & Frank argue that SWR content is multi-modal: retrieval-SWRs and consolidation-SWRs exist with different content characteristics, and may overlap within single events. For MECH-285 this suggests the staleness-priority signal likely operates specifically on consolidation-SWRs (the sleep-phase Bayesian aggregation arm -- MECH-275 consumer), while retrieval-SWRs (awake decision support) are priority-weighted by different signals (e.g. current task demand -- Mattar & Daw's need term). The architectural recommendation: MECH-285 should be a consumer-specific priority on a subset of SWR content, not a global replay-scheduling bias.
mapping.mapping_caveat: This is a review synthesising primary data, not primary data itself. It argues for multi-mode SWR function on the basis of published content studies; the strength of any specific MECH-285-relevant conclusion depends on the underlying studies. The review does not explicitly discuss epistemic-staleness priority or V_s residuals -- the mapping is inferential.
mapping.source_context: Review; synthesises rodent electrophysiology, closed-loop perturbation, and human SWR data up to mid-2018.

confidence_components.notes: High-quality review from leading authors on SWR content and function. Mapping fidelity moderate because MECH-285's specific staleness-priority architecture is not addressed, but the multi-mode framework the review establishes directly informs the MECH-285 implementation design.
